item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel a rebel joy when an author is heavily criticized for something and doesn't do anything about it. Shine on, you crazy diamond. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. In opposition to many of the folks who read this, I think it is much better and more otherworldly for the lack of explanation. Open up and dissect a fairy tale and its magic often bleeds out; this is a glimpse at someone else's world that leaves the specifics up to the reader's imagination.

item.title: Tale: Flora
item.content:encoded:
I actually ended up writing a tale about this, although my intentions may be different then the OP's.

Tale can be found here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "apparently from natural causes" would work, covering-one's-ass-wise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adding my voice to the general consensus that this needs some sort of an actual connection to the characters in the story to be effective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel that it is playing a bit too subtle. I love the imagery and the sentiment but there lacks any real feeling of connection from one image to the next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If a coroner doesn't suspect something else, he's probably not a Foundation coroner. "Unknown cause" would probably be best.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with that

I think that the proper way to say it would be something like "presumably of natural causes"

Anyway, I think that this has a lot of potential. Fix up the things that are unclear or don't sound right and maybe expand the story a little, give it more emotional impact.

Evidence gathered from SCP-2469-2 seem to indicate that SCP-2469-3 had lived there alone, however evidence was found that indicated SCP-2469-3 either received or expected to receive a single visitor on a regular basis.

I think you should go into more detail about this. Describe more about what was found inside of 2469-2 so that we can get a better idea of what kind of person 2469-3 was and what kind of preparations she made for her visitor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Death from old age" doesn't seem formal enough for a document.

This is true. That stopped being useful because it covered a broad range of chronic and acute ailments that can be brought on by old age. If it was related to general wear and tear, anything from a heart attack to kidney or liver failure would fall under that listing.

If a cause of death can't be determined (due to the state of the body,) and the coroner doesn't suspect something else as a cause of death, "natural causes" would be a better listing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one is nice and short with an okay concept. Not enough quality here to make me +1, but it is good enough to steer clear of a downvote. I only have one complaint.

…determined to be that of an unidentified female approximately eighty years of age at time of death from old age.

"Death from old age" doesn't seem formal enough for a document. Maybe "eighty years of age at time of death due to cardiac expiration"? Maybe that's too formal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with Roget and Djoric on this one, but I'm novoting. There are definitely things going on here that could be really great, but the romance angle isn't really played with very much before the very ending. It kinda left me guessing as to what exactly was going on, only to throw in a romance thing at the end that felt kind of abrupt. For me, it wasn't really given enough time to develop before the end.

However, it was well-written, and I liked the implications enough to novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's not enough here for me to be interested in what's going on, but I see potential for a great article. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think there's just enough here for an upvote, though a few more clues surrounding the two individuals and their relationship to the phenomena could make this into something truly memorable. Either way, something happened with the first sentence of that description, so you might want to check that again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't quite determine if this was a beautiful prison or a case of "I'll be right back" between two entities who experience time differently. The latter seems slightly more probable, but since either possibility could exist, I'm stuck at neutral without a little more to go on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought of Hades and Persephone. (fruit trees, fire, etc.)

Tbh I had something like this on the backburner, but I really like the otherworldly feel of this. Had a nice mental picture of the entire thing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sort of reminded me of the duck pond, but instead of having a clever trick behind it, it just didn't have much of interest at all. I agree with djkaktus above that it's hard to work with a romantic hook, if there's no insight on to why this love exists in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. I feel like the implications that are presented, and what they imply in particular, sort of present the story of the two in an indefinite, general sort of way, which I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this might work better as a tale, because as an article, it just feels… Flat. There are some weird tone things going on, and some of the effects feel forced (like bursting into flames spontaneously). I dunno. Maybe it's the complete lack of connection to the characters that does it. I feel like I should feel sympathy for the make character, but I don't.

-1, but I'd probably upvote a tale of this.

Edit: I've got it. It's like we walked in during the climax of a love story. You can't appreciate the climax if you've got no insight on the rest of the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find that any emotional impact this might have carried was crippled by the presentation. I would love to read about these two people, I would love to know their story, but I am prevented from any sort of emotional connection because the format requires distance. So the whole thing comes across as middling and disappointing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The imagery in this one is lovely. +1, although I can't help but wonder…isn't this a Serpent's Hand tale waiting to happen? :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why? It's neutralized.

Because the documentation is being kept for record-keeping, in case it recurs.

This is irrelevant. Sociable humans have visitors; it's not worthy of being included in an SCP report.

Sure, but a woman living alone in a cottage hundreds of miles away from anyone else in a valley that is inaccessible by ground transport and unknown to air travel is.

Although this would cause the Skip to be rather generic, this is an anomaly, meaning it can't be classified as neutralized.

You're not reading the entire article.

This is generic.

In what sense?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Critique
- Critique

Due to the remote location of SCP-2469-1, minimal containment are necessary

Grammatical error.

Samples and materials removed from SCP-2469 are stored at Site-42 for further analysis and research. Any deviations in the cycle of activity within SCP-2469-1 are to be reported immediately.

Why? It's neutralized.

This area is not accessible by ground vehicles and exhibits multiple anomalous properties, most notably that the center of SCP-2469-1 is an approximately 80 m radius clearing populated with flora inconsistent with the region, most notably a series of 13 fruit-bearing trees arranged into a sundial and various fruit-bearing or otherwise edible bushes and other plants arranged into various geometric and fractal patterns radiating outwards from the center of the clearing.

This is one sentence. One. Please fix this.

SCP-2469-2 is a small, apparently hand-constructed cottage built using only natural materials and located at the center of SCP-2469-1. Investigation of SCP-2469-2 discovered a single set of human remains (SCP-2469-3), determined to be that of an unidentified female approximately eighty years of age at time of death from old age and natural causes. Evidence gathered from SCP-2469-2 seem to indicate that SCP-2469-3 had lived there alone, however evidence was found that indicated SCP-2469-3 either received or expected to receive a single visitor on a regular basis.

This is irrelevant. Sociable humans have visitors; it's not worthy of being included in an SCP report.

SCP-2469-1 does not seem to be affected by changes in seasons, with all flowering plants within the area constantly in a state of full bloom and all fruit-bearing or edible plants in a constant state of being ready to harvest.

Although this would cause the Skip to be rather generic, this is an anomaly, meaning it can't be classified as neutralized.

During initial containment, an attempt was made to remove SCP-2469-3 from SCP-2469-2. When Foundation containment personnel attempted to remove the remains from the area, the area around SCP-2469-3 burst into flames, resulting in severe burns to several containment team personnel. Additionally, large wildlife native to the area became highly agitated and aggressive until the remains were returned to SCP-2469-2.

This is generic.

Overall, the concept was bland and your writing needs some considerable work.
-1
